questions.prompt: Which is the best alt text for a product photo of a blue leather shoe on a shoe retail site?

choices.text: alt="Blue leather oxford-style men's shoe, side view"
alt="image of blue shoe blue leather shoes best blue shoes 2026 buy shoes"

questions.explanation: Good alt text is descriptive, concise, and contextual. (a) is too vague to be useful. (c) is keyword-stuffed — doesn't help SEO, hurts accessibility. (d) is for purely decorative images, not product photos that carry real information. The right alt text describes what the image shows in context.

questions.prompt: When should you use an empty alt attribute (alt="")?

choices.text: For purely decorative images (icons, dividers, geometric patterns) that carry no meaning. Empty alt tells screen readers to skip them, which is the right behavior.
For all images, to avoid being penalized for over-optimization.
When you can't think of a good description.

questions.explanation: Decorative images should use alt="" so screen readers skip them rather than reading filler. Omitting the alt attribute entirely is worse — some screen readers will read the filename. Meaningful images always need real alt text.
